stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The popular “ocean garbage island” image suggests that floating plastic forms vast solid islands in the Pacific.
  • Oceanographer Dr. Lina Park first popularized this image in the early 2000s.
  • She described the area as “a plastic continent.”
  • In a 2023 study, marine debris specialist Dr. Carlos Vasquez and colleagues reviewed dozens of trawl surveys and aerial imaging campaigns.
  • Vasquez and colleagues concluded that media depictions of solid plastic islands are “a misleading shorthand at odds with the actual distribution of microplastics.”
stem: The student wants to use a quotation to emphasize a potential problem with the “ocean garbage island” image. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Describing the area as “a plastic continent,” Park first popularized the ocean garbage island image in the early 2000s.

After reviewing dozens of trawl surveys and aerial imaging campaigns, Vasquez and colleagues concluded that media depictions of solid plastic islands are “a misleading shorthand at odds with the actual distribution of microplastics.”
Vasquez and colleagues note that the topic of marine plastics “has captured the public imagination.”
Despite the concerns raised in Vasquez's 2023 study, the ocean garbage island image remains popular.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it uses a quotation from Vasquez and colleagues that directly identifies a problem with the “ocean garbage island” image (it's a “misleading shorthand”).

Choice A quotes a phrase that endorses the image. Choice C uses a quotation that doesn't identify a problem. Choice D doesn't use a quotation.
